LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF PAULA BOGANTES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST DEFENDANT ERNEST G. HOPE IS GRANTED AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES 3, 8, AND 13 BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS RAISED NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT OR IDENTIFIED ANY REASON TO THINK THERE IS ANY SUCH FACT TO BE FOUND. DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES EITHER BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT WITNESSES IDENTIFIED AS HAVING KNOWLEDGE DID NOT HAVE KNOWLEDGE, BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT THROUGH THE HOPE DECLARATION OR BECAUSE THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS NOT DEPENDENT ON PLEADING ANY FACTS. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS TO PREPARE AND ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

Notice Of Motion To Compel Df'S Further Responses To Written Discovery And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Dfs And Their Counsel Brian Van Vleck; Vol 1-3 Of Exhibits In Support Of Gregory M. Sheffer
Off Calendar Jun-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:

Gregory M. Sheffer, Esq., for Paula Bogantes, Plaintiff/Moving Party
Daniel Turner, Esq., for Ernest G. Hope, Defendant/Opposing Party (telephonic
appearance)

Hearing Held and Tentative Ruling Adopted and modified: Plaintiff Paula Bogantes' Motion To Compel Defendants' Further Responses To Written Discovery is granted. Defendant is ordered to respond further within 20 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. Late opposition has been considered.

Modification: Respond by July 6, 2007. EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion Andmotion To Compel The Deposition Of Deft Ernest G. Hope And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft Enrnest G. Hope And His Counsel, Carlton Disante & Freudenberger Llp
Off Calendar May-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Brian Van Vleck, appearing (via telephone) for Defendant/opposing party: Ernest G. Hope
No other appearance.

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Paul Bogantes's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant Ernest G. Hope is granted. Defendant is ordered to appear for his deposition and to produce the documents requested by the notice of deposition on a date to be agreed upon by counsel, but no later than May 31, 2007. The Request For Production of Documents referenced in the opposition and the Notice of Deposition and Document Demand are separate discovery devices. The failure to move to compel further responses to the Request For Production Of Documents does not preclude requesting those documents in the Notice Of Deposition. The court finds that Defendant's failure to appear was a misuse of the discovery process. This ruling does not make any findings as to objections that may be made to specific document demands at the deposition. Sanctions are imposed against Defendant in the amount of $1,440, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
